$comment: OpenClaw skill manifest for muhaven-rwa-skill. Mirrors the SKILL.md frontmatter in machine-readable form. ClawSecure permissions block is forward-compatible with NemoClaw runtime sandbox (OpenClaw issues #28298 / #28360). Network deny-by-default + filesystem deny-all + spawn deny-all is the lethal-trifecta defense (P8 forthcoming).
name: muhaven-rwa-skill
description: Confidential RWA portfolio agent on Fhenix CoFHE. Reads encrypted balances, stages buy + claim intents, never auto-submits. Three-tier confirmation surface (inline / Mini App OTP / passkey deep-link) keyed on intent notional.

openclaw_runtime.$comment_calendar_versioning: The npm-published `openclaw` package uses calendar versioning (e.g. 2026.5.7), not semver. The pre-2026-05-11 manifest assumed the NemoClaw-style semver range (0.4.0 / 0.5.x); that range is meaningless against the actual runtime. Range below pins the calendar major-minor at which §3 Path (a) A.4 was validated. Future-runtime adopters should bump `min` to the lowest calendar version that ships the `mcp.command/args` + `${__dirname}` template resolver the skill relies on.

network.$comment_egress_advisory_in_host_native: Allowlist intentionally pins production origins only — the published artifact is prod-targeted. Staging rehearsals (§3 Path (a) A.3-A.7 in AGENTIC_TEST_PLAN.md) install the same signed tarball with a `user_config.backend_url=https://api-stage.muhaven.app` override and rely on plain OpenClaw's `sandbox.fallback: host_native` posture (manifest permissions become advisory metadata, not kernel-enforced). Defense-in-depth in host_native mode lives in the bin shim's deny-default behavior + the broker daemon's JWT-scope check. A future kernel-enforced runtime would reject the staging override; that is intentional — production artifact stays production-pure.

sandbox.$comment_forward_compat: Per OpenClaw issues #28298 (permissions schema) and #28360 (sandbox boot ABI). When the runtime doesn't recognize a field, it MUST treat unknown permissions as deny — not allow. Verified on NemoClaw 0.5.0 release notes.
sandbox.$comment_runtime_decision: Operator runtime decision 2026-05-11 (c8aba6d) shifted §3 Path (a) A.4 from NemoClaw to plain OpenClaw via `npm install -g openclaw@latest`. The `runtime: nemoclaw` field above stays as the preferred runtime claim; `fallback: host_native` is what actually runs today. Sandbox + permissions become advisory in host_native mode. Promote to NemoClaw once the homelab is willing to host the NVIDIA OpenShell + GPU stack.

mcp.$comment_args_template_resolver: `${__dirname}` is the MCPB-canonical template token (resolves to the skill's install-time root). Plain OpenClaw's manifest resolver behavior is empirically verified during §3 Path (a) A.4 (operator-driven). The bin shim is the launch target (NOT `dist/index.cjs` directly) because it wraps `runOpenClawSkill()` in `.then(...) .catch(err => process.stderr.write("fatal: " + err.stack))` — direct-launch of `dist/index.cjs` would crash silently on an unhandled rejection. If a runtime fails to expand `${__dirname}`, the `runtime.entry_point: dist/index.cjs` field at manifest line 40 is the documented fallback hook.

env.$comment_skill_version_env: MUHAVEN_OPENCLAW_SKILL_VERSION is intentionally NOT pre-set here. The bin shim's runOpenClawSkill() does `process.env.MUHAVEN_OPENCLAW_SKILL_VERSION = resolveSkillVersion()` at boot — the value comes from the `__SKILL_VERSION__` tsup-injected build-time constant (which itself reads `packages/openclaw-skill/package.json#version`). Hardcoding a literal here was the 5th drift site; the bin override always wins and a stale literal here only confuses install-time inspection.

tools.name: muhaven.read.protection_coverage
tools.description: Read DefaultProtection coverage for an RWA token (reserveRateBps + status). Public aggregates only.

tools.name: muhaven.read.kyc_attestation
tools.description: Read-only informational explainer for MuHaven cross-chain KYC attestations.

tools.name: muhaven.position.buy
tools.description: Stage a Subscription buy — emits intent for three-tier confirmation. NEVER auto-submits.

tools.name: muhaven.position.claim
tools.description: Stage a yield claim — emits intent for three-tier confirmation. NEVER auto-submits.

user_config.title: MuHaven dashboard URL
user_config.description: Dashboard origin used by deep-link confirmations and the device-code authorization page (/link). Hardcoded for phishing resistance — do not change unless you know what you are doing.

user_config.title: muhaven-broker IPC endpoint
user_config.description: Path to the broker's Unix socket (POSIX) or named pipe (Windows). Leave blank for the per-user default.

user_config.title: Read-only mode
user_config.description: When enabled, only the muhaven.read.* toolset is exposed; position.* and policy.* tools are not registered.
